note: Restructured: AI-foundations-first front (4 learning weeks), interleaved learn-then-build, reslip to Jun 29 2026. Source of truth; ICS rendered from this.

2026-06-29.title: 📖 Read Anthropic 'Building Effective Agents' end to end; take structured notes

2026-06-30.title: 📖 Read the ReAct paper (Yao et al.); diagram the reason-act-observe loop by hand

2026-07-01.title: 📖 Study the six patterns conceptually: prompt-chaining, routing, parallelization, orchestrator-workers, evaluator-optimizer, autonomous agent. No code yet.

2026-07-02.title: 📖 For each pattern, write 3 sentences: what it is, when it earns its complexity, a real example. This is the judgment you will build on.

2026-07-03.title: 🔮 Prediction note (PRIVATE): 'What I think an agent actually is' - your prior BEFORE building. Be bold, be wrong; the value is staking a position so you feel the correction later. Not an essay, not graded, not for posting.

2026-07-06.title: 🔨 A1.1: scaffold the six patterns as swappable modules sharing one interface

2026-07-07.title: 🔨 A1.1: implement prompt-chaining + routing + parallelization against one shared task

2026-07-08.title: 🔨 A1.1: implement orchestrator-workers + evaluator-optimizer + the autonomous-agent loop

2026-07-09.title: 🔨 A1.1: run all six on the same task, measure (tokens, latency, quality); tabulate

2026-07-10.title: ✍️ A1.1 write-up: when each pattern earns its complexity, backed by YOUR numbers (this is the deliverable, not the demos)

2026-07-13.title: 📖 LLM mechanics: context windows, tokenization, sampling/temperature; how they shape agent behavior

2026-07-14.title: 📖 Tool use / function calling deeply: schemas, the model's decision to call, error handling

2026-07-15.title: 📖 DDD intro: strategic vs tactical, why domain modeling matters for agentic systems (clean boundaries = clean tools)

2026-07-16.title: 📖 DDD: event storming as a technique; read the A2.1 spec and an event-storming primer

2026-07-17.title: 🔮 Prediction note (PRIVATE): 'Why an agent system might be a domain-modeling problem' - your hunch connecting the AI + DDD threads, written before you have built either. A prior to be corrected, not a polished claim.

2026-07-20.title: 📖 Eval methodology: why agents need evals, LLM-as-judge, the assertion mindset; read Hamel Husain / Eugene Yan

2026-07-21.title: 📖 Retrieval basics: keyword vs semantic, what BM25 does and why; read the A1.4 spec

2026-07-22.title: 🔨 A1.4: implement BM25 by hand on a small corpus (the no-magic retrieval grounding)

2026-07-23.title: 📖 Connect it forward: how eval + retrieval will show up in the Curator (the brain's triage, A3.1's harness)

2026-07-24.title: 🔮 Prediction note (PRIVATE): 'How I think I will know if my agent is any good' - your eval intuitions before studying eval methodology. Predict; you will check it against reality in M3.

2026-07-27.title: 🔨 M0: deploy the base Curator (FastAPI on Fly, /health) - the skeleton

2026-07-28.title: 🔨 M0: hand-roll the ReAct loop, NOW understanding every line because you studied it in wk0-1

2026-07-29.title: 🔨 M0: the two tools (read_program deterministic + fetch_rss) with acceptance tests

2026-07-30.title: 🔨 M0: the cron digest (two sections) + Postgres (feeds + seen_items) + README

2026-07-31.title: ✍️ Essay (publishable): revisit your wk0 prediction note now that you have built M0. The pre/post DELTA is the essay - what I believed, what building taught me, why I was wrong. THIS is downstream of the work, so it meets the essay standard.

2026-08-03.title: 🔨 A2.1: event-storm the Curator's own domain (the technique from wk2, applied for real)

2026-08-04.title: 🔨 A2.1: cluster events into candidate bounded contexts; classify core/supporting/generic

2026-08-05.title: 🔨 A2.1: ubiquitous-language glossary + a hand-drawn context map (no AI)

2026-08-06.title: 🔨 Harden the M0 digest cron; make the two sources fail independently

2026-08-07.title: ✍️ Decision memo: the bounded contexts you found and why (feeds the A2.2 refactor)

2026-08-10.title: 🔨 A3.1: build the eval harness (>=20 assertions) - the methodology you studied wk3, now real

2026-08-11.title: 🔨 A3.1: hand-grade 10 outputs, align the judge to your labels (the alignment notebook)

2026-08-12.title: 🔨 A7.1: the async queue + workers (surfaced items fan out; skipped never enqueue)

2026-08-13.title: 🔨 A7.1: REST API over the queue + a 15-min queue decision memo

2026-08-14.title: ✍️ Write-up: what the eval harness caught that you would have missed by eyeballing

2026-08-17.title: 🔨 P5: seed import (specs.json + program-calendar.ics), verified counts + completion CLI

2026-08-19.title: 🔨 P5: served /calendar.ics (stable UIDs!) + the digest reads /todos/today

2026-08-21.title: ✍️ Reflection: the Curator now owns its own program. What did closing that loop teach you?

2026-08-25.title: 🔨 A1.2: surfaced-with-reasons citing program state + the writing-post relevance join

2026-08-26.title: 🔨 A1.2: decision tracing (JSONL) feeding A3.1 + degraded-mode fallback

2026-08-28.title: ✍️ The interview sentence, earned: 'an agent that knows my study program and filters the field through it.' Write the 2-min version.

2026-09-04.title: ✍️ Before/after: the Curator's domain model pre- and post-DDD-refactor. What got clearer?
